subject: Cloud Computing
title: Google's Engineering-First Approach
description: A micro-lesson that makes Google's Engineering-First Approach usable.

hook.text: Google built the systems that handle 8.5 billion searches a day. Then it tried to sell that technology.

buildup.text: Google Cloud Platform launched in 2008 with App Engine, targeting developers who valued technology above all.

discovery.text: GCP offers arguably the best data analytics, AI, and Kubernetes services in the market.

twist.text: But technology alone doesn't win enterprise deals. Google lacked the sales teams and support enterprises demand.

climax.text: GCP holds about 11% of the market — a distant third. Brilliant tech, weaker go-to-market execution.

punchline.text: Google built the best tech. Selling it was the hard part.

quiz.question: Why is Google Cloud third in market share despite strong technology?
options: It lacked the enterprise sales teams and support that AWS and Azure built
Google Cloud is only available in the United States
Its services are too expensive for any business to afford
